# Pattern classification — synthesizer-bug-r1

Per M4.5.E7-PLAN.md § S1.t2 + RESEARCH.md § 3, each documented R1 character-drop pattern is classified by determinism class. The classification drives which test layer covers each pattern:

- **Layer B** — Deterministic at the synthesizer seam. The source content is known verbatim (a heading literal in `commands/init.md`, or a section body extractable from a scanner output). A new `embedSection` helper + heading-literal assertion can guarantee preservation.
- **Layer C** — Free-form prose / shape property. The bug emerges from LLM generation latitude. A prompt-template lint test can catch the anti-pattern shape that produced it (long lines, missing newline before code-fence, etc.).

---

## Classification table

| # | Pattern | Buggy form | Source | Class | Test layer | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Heading boundary — Inferred goals | `## Ierred goals & uncertainties` | `commands/init.md` Step 3 template, literal h2 heading `## Inferred goals & uncertainties` | **Layer B** | S1.t4 (heading-literal + round-trip) | Heading is a literal in init.md. Layer B test asserts: (a) `expected/LANDSCAPE.md` has the exact h2 line; (b) `extractSection(content, 'Inferred goals & uncertainties')` returns non-null for `expected/`; (c) the same regex does NOT match `actual/` (RED-state assertion). |
| 2 | Heading boundary — Constraints | `## ints` | `commands/init.md` Step 4 template, literal h2 heading `## Constraints` | **Layer B** | S1.t4 (heading-literal + round-trip) | Same shape as #1. Heading literal in init.md PROJECT.md template. Round-trip via `extractSection`. |
| 3 | Table-cell drop — `index.js` row | `\| is \| Top-level entry (224 bytes)` | `tests/fixtures/synthesizer-bug-r1/scan/structure.md` § Source Tree (depth-3) — to be embedded verbatim by the synthesizer | **Layer B** | S1.t5 (`embedSection` helper) + S1.t6 (template references helper) | The structure scan's Source Tree table is supposed to be embedded verbatim per init.md Step 3 § Project structure. New `embedSection(scanContent, 'Source Tree (depth-3)')` helper returns the literal substring; init.md template stops asking the LLM to "embed verbatim" (which can drop chars) and instead calls the helper. |
| 4 | Command-flag drop — `--check-leaks` | `--checkt/ test/acceptance/` | `tests/fixtures/synthesizer-bug-r1/scan/quality.md` § Test Runners — literal Mocha CLI in `package.json scripts.test` | **Layer B** | S1.t5 (`embedSection`-style extract) + S1.t6 (template) | Borderline B/C: the command is deterministic-from-scan if the synthesizer embeds rather than paraphrases. Lean Layer B because the quality scan emits the command literally; the fix is the same shape as #3 — extract scan section verbatim instead of asking the LLM to reproduce it. If init.md template doesn't currently embed test-runner detail verbatim, S1.t6 amendment makes it do so. |
| 5 | Sentence/code-fence boundary | `...not real cadence).git fetch --unshallow\`` (missing newline + backtick) | Free-form prose generated by synthesizer when phrasing the shallow-clone remediation hint in Activity signals | **Layer C** | S1.t7 (template lint — no sentence-terminator immediately before code-fence opener without blank-line gap) | The synthesizer chose how to phrase the remediation hint. Bug is anti-pattern of jamming a fenced code block (`` `git fetch --unshallow` ``) directly against the preceding sentence-terminator (`).`). Layer C lint asserts that `commands/init.md` never positions a sentence-end character followed immediately by a backtick within the same line (a smell that propagates to LLM output). |
| 6 | Mid-word truncation in Notes | `Constraints (Team / contributoiteria.` | Free-form prose generated by synthesizer in PROJECT.md Notes when summarizing Constraints status | **Layer C** | S1.t7 (template lint — long-line / dense-prose anti-pattern) | Mid-word drop of `~12 chars from a long sentence — correlates with LLM truncation under dense generation. Layer C lint asserts `commands/init.md` template lines don't exceed N chars (default 200) so the model isn't pushed into truncation territory. |

---

## Summary

| Class | Count | Patterns | Coverage path |
|---|---|---|---|
| Layer B | 4 | #1, #2, #3, #4 | S1.t4 + S1.t5 + S1.t6 (new `embedSection` helper + heading-literal + round-trip tests) |
| Layer C | 2 | #5, #6 | S1.t7 + S1.t8 (template lint + minimal init.md anti-pattern fixes) |
| Total | 6 | — | — |

**Layer B share: 4/6 (67%).** Above the 50% threshold flagged in M4.5.E7-PLAN.md § S1.t2 ("If 50%+ are Layer C, surface a planning question"). No escalation needed.

The 4/2 split aligns with M4.5.E7-RESEARCH.md § 3's expectation that "deterministic seams (Layer B) carry the load; Layer C catches the prose-shape residue." Test count rough sizing per M4.5.E7-PLAN.md § Slice 1:
- S1.t4: 5+ Layer B tests for patterns #1, #2 + sibling heading boundaries (2-3 siblings)
- S1.t5: 3 unit tests for the new `embedSection` helper
- S1.t6: 1 Layer B integration test against init.md template referencing the helper
- S1.t7: 4-6 Layer C property tests against commands/init.md
- **Total estimate:** 13-15 new tests (matches RESEARCH § 3 estimate of 13-16).

---

## Sibling cases — heading boundaries to add to S1.t4

Beyond the documented patterns #1 + #2, S1.t4's "round-trip via extractSection" test will iterate every h2 heading in `expected/LANDSCAPE.md` + `expected/PROJECT.md`. Those headings are the implicit sibling-coverage set:

- LANDSCAPE.md: `What this project is`, `Tech stack`, `Project structure`, `Activity signals`, `Test surface`, `Open work signals`, `Inferred goals & uncertainties`, `Last Updated` (8 headings)
- PROJECT.md: `Vision`, `Problem Statement`, `Success Criteria`, `Scope`, `Constraints`, `Done When`, `Notes` (7 headings)

15 total h2 headings; each becomes an implicit sibling assertion in the round-trip test. Combined with patterns #1 + #2 as explicit individual assertions, S1.t4 will land at ~5 named tests + 1 parametrized round-trip = effectively ~17 heading-boundary assertions for the price of 6 test cases.

---

*Created 2026-05-23 as part of M4.5.E7.S1.t2. Drives test design in S1.t4 (Layer B) and S1.t7 (Layer C).*
